title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: db object lightweight connection: holds variant trait tables memory reads z-scores demand binary chunks.
code: library(pleiodbr) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") db pleiodb database   path:           /path/to/main.pleiodb   format version: 3   variants (V):   95,378   traits (T):     4,159   chunk shape:    512×512

what: PheWAS — one variant across all traits
title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: phewas() fetches z-scores single variant across every trait. can query exact ALID (chrom:pos_REF_ALT) genomic region.
code: # rs429358 region (APOE ε4 proxy) — affects LDL, Alzheimer's risk, and more pw <- phewas(db, "19:45412079_C_T") pw

previous_headings: PheWAS — one variant across all traits
title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: # Annotate with trait names from db$traits pw <- pw |>   left_join(db$traits[, c("trait_id", "trait_name")], by = "trait_id") |>   mutate(     log10p   = -log10(pval),     highlight = pval < 5e-8   ) |>   arrange(log10p)  # Assign a numeric x position per trait (sorted by p-value for clarity) pw$x <- seq_len(nrow(pw))  ggplot(pw, aes(x = x, y = log10p, colour = highlight)) +   geom_point(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(5e-8), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "steelblue", "TRUE" = "firebrick"),                       guide = "none") +   ggrepel::geom_text_repel(     data = filter(pw, pval < 1e-20),     aes(label = trait_name),     size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 12   ) +   labs(     x = "Trait (ranked by p-value)",     y = expression(-log[10](p)),     title = "PheWAS: 19:45412079_C_T"   ) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)

what: GWAS — all variants for one trait
title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: gwas() scans entire trait column, returning non-missing associations.
code: bmi <- gwas(db, "ukb-b-19953")   # Body mass index (BMI), N ≈ 461,460 bmi

previous_headings: GWAS — all variants for one trait
title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: # Parse chromosome and position from ALID bmi <- bmi |>   mutate(     chrom = as.integer(sub(":.*", "", variant_id)),     pos   = as.numeric(sub(".*:(\\d+)_.*", "\\1", variant_id)),     log10p = -log10(pval)   ) |>   filter(!is.na(chrom)) |>   arrange(chrom, pos)  # Build x-axis offset per chromosome chrom_offsets <- bmi |>   group_by(chrom) |>   summarise(max_pos = max(pos), .groups = "drop") |>   arrange(chrom) |>   mutate(offset = lag(cumsum(as.numeric(max_pos)), default = 0))  bmi <- bmi |>   left_join(chrom_offsets[, c("chrom", "offset")], by = "chrom") |>   mutate(gpos = pos + offset)  # Chromosome midpoints for x-axis labels chrom_labels <- bmi |>   group_by(chrom) |>   summarise(mid = mean(gpos), .groups = "drop")  ggplot(bmi, aes(x = gpos, y = log10p,                 colour = factor(chrom %% 2),                 shape  = ifelse(imputed, "imputed", "observed"))) +   geom_point(size = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(5e-8), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey30") +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("0" = "#3A7DC9", "1" = "#B0C4DE"), guide = "none") +   scale_shape_manual(values = c("observed" = 16, "imputed" = 17),                      name = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = chrom_labels$mid, labels = chrom_labels$chrom) +   labs(     x = "Chromosome",     y = expression(-log[10](p)),     title = "GWAS Manhattan — Body mass index (BMI)"   ) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: tophits() returns genome-wide-significant associations set traits, using pre-built index speed.
code: hits <- tophits(   db,   traits = c("ukb-b-19953",       # BMI              "ebi-a-GCST006867"), # Type 2 diabetes   pval   = 5e-8 )  hits |>   left_join(db$traits[, c("trait_id","trait_name")], by = "trait_id") |>   count(trait_name, sort = TRUE)

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: associations() fetches specific set variant–trait pairs. workhorse Mendelian randomisation.
code: # A handful of LDL instruments (near APOE, PCSK9, LDLR) ldl_instruments <- c(   "19:45412079_C_T",   # APOE region   "1:55496039_G_T",    # PCSK9   "19:11200038_C_T",   # LDLR region   "2:21231524_T_C"     # APOB region )  assoc <- associations(   db,   variants = ldl_instruments,   traits   = c("ebi-a-GCST90018961",  # LDL cholesterol (exposure)                "ebi-a-GCST003116")    # Coronary artery disease (outcome) ) assoc

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: use associations run IVW MR-Egger TwoSampleMR.
code: library(TwoSampleMR)  # Pivot into exposure/outcome data frames exposure <- assoc |>   filter(trait_id == "ebi-a-GCST90018961") |>   transmute(     SNP    = variant_id,     beta.exposure = beta,     se.exposure   = se,     pval.exposure = pval,     eaf.exposure  = eaf,     effect_allele.exposure  = sub(".*_([A-Z])_[A-Z]$", "\\1", variant_id),     other_allele.exposure   = sub(".*_[A-Z]_([A-Z])$", "\\1", variant_id),     exposure = "LDL cholesterol",     id.exposure = "ebi-a-GCST90018961",     mr_keep.exposure = TRUE   )  outcome <- assoc |>   filter(trait_id == "ebi-a-GCST003116") |>   transmute(     SNP    = variant_id,     beta.outcome = beta,     se.outcome   = se,     pval.outcome = pval,     eaf.outcome  = eaf,     effect_allele.outcome = sub(".*_([A-Z])_[A-Z]$", "\\1", variant_id),     other_allele.outcome  = sub(".*_[A-Z]_([A-Z])$", "\\1", variant_id),     outcome = "Coronary artery disease",     id.outcome = "ebi-a-GCST003116",     mr_keep.outcome = TRUE   )  dat <- harmonise_data(exposure, outcome, action = 2) res <- mr(dat) res[, c("method", "nsnp", "b", "se", "pval")]

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: ggplot(dat, aes(x = beta.exposure, y = beta.outcome)) +   geom_point(     aes(colour = ifelse(mr_keep, "keep", "excluded")),     size = 3   ) +   geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = beta.outcome - se.outcome,                     ymax = beta.outcome + se.outcome),                 width = 0, alpha = 0.5) +   geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = beta.exposure - se.exposure,                      xmax = beta.exposure + se.exposure),                  height = 0, alpha = 0.5) +   # IVW slope   geom_abline(     slope     = res$b[res$method == "Inverse variance weighted"],     intercept = 0,     colour    = "steelblue", linewidth = 1   ) +   # MR-Egger slope and intercept   geom_abline(     slope     = res$b[res$method == "MR Egger"],     intercept = mr_pleiotropy_test(dat)$egger_intercept,     colour    = "firebrick", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1   ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("keep" = "black", "excluded" = "grey70"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   ggrepel::geom_text_repel(     aes(label = SNP), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 10   ) +   labs(     x     = "β LDL cholesterol (per SD)",     y     = "β Coronary artery disease (log OR)",     title = "MR: LDL cholesterol → Coronary artery disease",     caption = "Blue = IVW; Red dashed = MR-Egger"   ) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: rho() retrieves pairwise phenotypic correlations estimated sample overlap rho matrix.
code: # Correlation between BMI and Type 2 diabetes rho(db, "ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867")

what: Correlation heatmap for a trait cluster
title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: library(tidyr)  metabolic_traits <- c(   "ukb-b-19953",       # BMI   "ebi-a-GCST006867",  # Type 2 diabetes   "ebi-a-GCST90018961",# LDL cholesterol   "ebi-a-GCST90002412",# LDL (larger study)   "ebi-a-GCST003116"   # Coronary artery disease )  rho_mat <- rho(db, metabolic_traits, metabolic_traits)  # Label by trait name trait_labels <- db$traits |>   filter(trait_id %in% metabolic_traits) |>   select(trait_id, trait_name)  rho_mat <- rho_mat |>   left_join(trait_labels, by = c("trait_id_1" = "trait_id")) |>   rename(name1 = trait_name) |>   left_join(trait_labels, by = c("trait_id_2" = "trait_id")) |>   rename(name2 = trait_name)  ggplot(rho_mat, aes(x = name1, y = name2, fill = rho)) +   geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_text(aes(label = round(rho, 2)), size = 3) +   scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#4575B4", mid = "white", high = "#D73027",                        midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "ρ") +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = "Phenotypic correlations") +   theme_bw(base_size = 10) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: query functions return imputed column flagging z-scores produced LD reference completion rather direct GWAS. MR may want check whether instruments imputed: Imputed z-scores slightly wider uncertainty constrained observed |z| range surrounding LD block, treat caution soft imputed genotypes.
code: assoc |>   select(variant_id, trait_id, z, pval, imputed) |>   arrange(desc(imputed))

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: pull associations locus — useful colocalisation regional plots — combine region-string PheWAS associations():
code: # All variants in the FTO locus (BMI lead region on chr16) fto_variants <- phewas(db, "16:53e6-54e6") |>   filter(trait_id == "ukb-b-19953") |>   arrange(pval)  # Regional Manhattan for BMI at FTO ggplot(fto_variants, aes(   x     = as.numeric(sub(".*:(\\d+)_.*", "\\1", variant_id)) / 1e6,   y     = -log10(pval),   colour = imputed,   shape  = imputed )) +   geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "steelblue", "TRUE" = "#E07B39"),                       labels = c("Observed", "Imputed")) +   scale_shape_manual(values  = c("FALSE" = 16, "TRUE" = 17),                      labels  = c("Observed", "Imputed")) +   labs(     x      = "Position on chr16 (Mb)",     y      = expression(-log[10](p)),     colour = NULL, shape = NULL,     title  = "FTO locus — BMI (chr16:53–54 Mb)"   ) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

text: Gibran Hemani. Author, maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {pleiodbr: Read pleiodb GWAS Databases from R},   author = {Gibran Hemani},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://explodecomputer.github.io/pleiodbr/}, }

title: pleiodbr
text: R reader pleiodb — binary database format storing GWAS z-scores across thousands traits genome-wide scale. pleiodbr reads .pleiodb databases directly R Python dependency. supports PheWAS, GWAS, top-hits, arbitrary variant×trait block queries, phenotypic correlation (rho) queries. queries return tidy tibbles.

title: pleiodbr
text: zstdlite dependency R-universe (yet CRAN). hit zstdlite installation error, install first: Dependencies: zstdlite, tibble, dplyr, jsonlite.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("explodecomputer/pleiodbr") install.packages("zstdlite", repos = c("https://coolbutuseless.r-universe.dev",                                         "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

title: pleiodbr
code: library(pleiodbr)  # Open a database db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") print(db) #> pleiodb database #>   path:           /path/to/main.pleiodb #>   format version: 3 #>   variants (V):   95,378 #>   traits (T):     4,159 #>   chunk shape:    512×512  # PheWAS — one variant across all traits phewas(db, "16:53800954_C_T")      # exact ALID phewas(db, "16:53e6-54e6")         # genomic region  # GWAS — all variants for one trait gwas(db, "ukb-b-19953")            # Body mass index  # Top hits tophits(db, traits = c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867"), pval = 5e-8)  # Arbitrary variant × trait block associations(   db,   variants = c("16:53800954_C_T", "19:45412079_C_T"),   traits   = c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867") )  # Phenotypic correlation rho(db, "ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867")

title: pleiodbr
text: query functions except rho() return tibble : rho() returns long-format tibble columns trait_id_1, trait_id_2, rho.

title: pleiodbr
text: .pleiodb directory stores V×T matrix GWAS z-scores (variants × traits) zstd-compressed 512×512 int16 chunks. also stores effective sample sizes (Neff), pairwise phenotypic correlations (rho), optional imputed z-scores produced LD reference completion. Python pleiodb package builds databases OpenGWAS VCF files.

title: pleiodbr
text: See vignette("pleiodbr") full walkthrough including Manhattan plots, PheWAS plots, Mendelian randomisation example.

title: Associations block query — associations
text: Returns association statistics arbitrary set variants × traits.

title: Associations block query — associations
code: associations(db, variants, traits)

title: Associations block query — associations
text: db pleiodb object open_pleiodb(). variants Character vector ALID strings. traits Character vector OpenGWAS trait IDs.

title: Associations block query — associations
text: tibble columns variant_id, trait_id, z, beta, se, pval, eaf, n, imputed. Rows z = NA dropped.

title: Associations block query — associations
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") associations(db,   variants = c("16:53800954_C_T", "19:45412079_C_T"),   traits   = c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867")) } # }

title: Read a rectangular block from a chunked matrix file. — .get_block
text: Read rectangular block chunked matrix file.

title: Read a rectangular block from a chunked matrix file. — .get_block
code: .get_block(db, name, v_start, v_end, t_start, t_end)

title: Read a rectangular block from a chunked matrix file. — .get_block
text: db pleiodb S3 object name matrix name without extension ("zscore", "neff", "rho") v_start, v_end row range [v_start, v_end) t_start, t_end column range [t_start, t_end)

title: Read a rectangular block from a chunked matrix file. — .get_block
text: numeric matrix (v_end-v_start) × (t_end-t_start), decoded

title: GWAS query — gwas
text: Returns association statistics variants single trait.

title: GWAS query — gwas
code: gwas(db, trait_id)

title: GWAS query — gwas
text: db pleiodb object open_pleiodb(). trait_id OpenGWAS trait ID string.

title: GWAS query — gwas
text: tibble columns variant_id, trait_id, z, beta, se, pval, eaf, n, imputed. Rows z = NA dropped.

title: GWAS query — gwas
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ db  <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") gwas(db, "ukb-b-19953") } # }

title: Open a pleiodb database — open_pleiodb
text: Reads metadata .pleiodb directory returns connection object. query functions (phewas, gwas, tophits, associations, rho) accept object first argument.

title: Open a pleiodb database — open_pleiodb
code: open_pleiodb(path)

title: Open a pleiodb database — open_pleiodb
text: path Path .pleiodb directory.

title: Open a pleiodb database — open_pleiodb
text: S3 object class "pleiodb".

title: Open a pleiodb database — open_pleiodb
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") print(db) } # }

title: PheWAS query — phewas
text: Returns association statistics single variant (variants genomic region) across every trait database.

title: PheWAS query — phewas
code: phewas(db, variant)

title: PheWAS query — phewas
text: db pleiodb object open_pleiodb(). variant Either ALID string ("1:103574777_C_G") region string ("1:103e6-104e6", positions base-pairs).

title: PheWAS query — phewas
text: tibble columns variant_id, trait_id, z, beta, se, pval, eaf, n, imputed. Rows z = NA dropped.

title: PheWAS query — phewas
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") phewas(db, "16:53800954_C_T") phewas(db, "16:53e6-54e6") } # }

title: Rho (phenotypic correlation) query — rho
text: Returns pairwise phenotypic correlations sets traits sample-overlap-weighted rho matrix.

title: Rho (phenotypic correlation) query — rho
code: rho(db, traits_1, traits_2)

title: Rho (phenotypic correlation) query — rho
text: db pleiodb object open_pleiodb(). traits_1 Character vector OpenGWAS trait IDs. traits_2 Character vector OpenGWAS trait IDs.

title: Rho (phenotypic correlation) query — rho
text: tibble columns trait_id_1, trait_id_2, rho covering cross-product pairs. Use tidyr::pivot_wider() matrix form.

title: Rho (phenotypic correlation) query — rho
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") rho(db, "ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867") rho(db, c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST90018961"), c("ebi-a-GCST006867")) } # }

title: Top hits query — tophits
text: Returns significant variant-trait associations p-value threshold specified set traits.

title: Top hits query — tophits
code: tophits(db, traits, pval = 5e-08)

title: Top hits query — tophits
text: db pleiodb object open_pleiodb(). traits Character vector OpenGWAS trait IDs. Required. pval P-value threshold (default 5e-8). database stores pre-built masks 5e-8 1e-5; thresholds trigger full-column scan.

title: Top hits query — tophits
text: tibble columns variant_id, trait_id, z, beta, se, pval, eaf, n, imputed.

title: Top hits query — tophits
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") tophits(db, traits = "ukb-b-19953") tophits(db, traits = c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867"), pval = 1e-5) } # }
